steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what "reaching across to" most nearly means in context. Mrs. Wilkins stays in her seat and physically extends her arm toward a bowl of oranges to take one. The correct answer must capture that physical act of extending toward something.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Joining with.': "Joining with" implies association or combining with another person or group. Mrs. Wilkins is not joining anything; she is extending her arm toward an object. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Gaining on.': "Gaining on" means closing a distance over time, as in chasing something. Mrs. Wilkins performs a single brief motion, not a chase or pursuit. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Stretching toward.': This precisely captures reaching while remaining seated — extending one's arm or body in the direction of something without moving to it. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Arriving at.': "Arriving at" describes reaching a destination after travel. Mrs. Wilkins explicitly stays where she is; she does not go to the bowl. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
